headline: The Death Penalty Repeal That Stopped Halfway
context: Parliament replaced mandatory death sentences with judicial discretion in 2023, but 1,340 prisoners remain on death row under the old law.

cards.big: Mandatory death penalty abolished. 1,340 people still sentenced under it.
Dangerous Drugs Act Section 39B carried mandatory death until 2023.
cards.sub: Judges had no discretion. Couriers received the same sentence as kingpins.

cards.big: 68% of death row inmates are drug offenders. 42% are foreign nationals.
cards.sub: The policy disproportionately executed the lowest-level participants.

cards.big: Resentencing requires individual hearings for all 1,340 cases.
cards.sub: Courts allocated 12 judges. At current pace, resentencing completes in 2031.

cards.big: If the old law was unjust enough to repeal, what about those sentenced under it?
cards.sub: Justice delayed for death row inmates is not an administrative inconvenience.

cards.big: The principle was settled in 2023. The implementation betrays it. Allocate the resources or admit the reform was performative.
